frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Honestly, if a woman says no these days it can mean many things, so obviously a man will first understand whether she is already committed or not. Once you know she has a husband or boyfriend, you should step back and respect that boundary properly. Otherwise people misread normal interest as harassment.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats a woman's own refusal as unclear or negotiable, while framing a male partner's claim as the real boundary that must be respected. It encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning she is 'taken' and using respectability language to justify it.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
